person.name: Robert de Everingham II

burialPlace.address: St Michael the Archangel Churchyard, Laxton, Nottinghamshire, England

person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Robert de Everingham of Laxton, Notts, Everingham, Kipling Cotes and Sherburn, co. York, Westborough, co. Lincoln, & c.
b.1256, Sherburn, Yorkshire, d. shortly before 21 July 1287, bur. St Michael's church. Laxton, Nottinghamshire.

Sir Robert de Everingham was the son and heir of Adam de Everingham and his wife Maude, the daughter of Sir John Monceaux of Barmston. Sir Robert de Everingham was of Everingham, Yorkshire and Laxton and Shelford in Nottinghamshire. Sir Robert de Everingham son and heir Adam de Everingham (1279-1341) succeded him as Lord of Laxton. Sir Robert de Everingham's tomb and effigy can be found in St. Michael's Church in the village of Laxton, Nottinghamshire.

«b»Parents«/b»
Sir Adam de Everingham (d.08 Dec 1280) and Isabella Monceaux (1234-1270) but note that the non-sourced findagrave annotation above names her as Maude not Isabel.

Father: Sir Adam de Everingham Knight, 1231-1280 (d. Conception of the Blessed Mary 9 Edward I = 8 Dec 1280.
A writ was sent to Richard de Holebrok, the king's steward, on 28 December 9 Edward I, after which Inquisitions were taken in Lincoln, York, and Nottingham, which found that his first born son and heir was Robert, aged 24.
Robert was the oldest son and heir of Adam de Everingham;

Marriage
m. in or before 1271 Lady Alice de La Hyde Baroness Everingham 1257-1326

«b»Issue:«/b»
Sir Adam de Everingham (b.1279/80 Sherburn; heir)

«b»Children:«/b»
1.) Sir Adam (1279-<1341)

2.) Robert (-1316)

3.) Thomas (->1312)

«b»Property«/b»
9 Feb 1280/1: did homage and had livery of father's lands, age 24.

«b»Occupation«/b»
Military: 1282 - on duty in Wales under Henry de Laci; 6 Apr 1282 to 14 Mar 1282 attend King at Shrewsbury on 28 Jun 1283; 1287 duty probably died in service

Military Council: 14 Jun 1287 by writs to Roberto de Everingham.
sometime hereditary Keeper of Sherwood Forest.

«b»Death«/b»
Robert died shortly before 21 July 1287. On that day his widow was given the manor of Everingham to hold in tenancy till her dower was assigned, i.e., till 11 Sep following.

Awrit was sent to the escheator this side of Trent on 21 July in 15 Edward I [1287], regarding Robert de Everingham, and Extents and/or Inquisitions were taken in Lincoln, Nottingham and York, which found that Robert's son Adam, aged 7 or 8 years, was his heir. William le Mascy held 1/16 knight's fee, Alexander de Le held 1/16 in Gedling, Nottingham, of Robert de Everingham.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Robert de Everingham II於1256年2月9日出生在英國英格蘭越可爾費艾爾本時，他的父親Adam de Everingham I26歲，母親Maude de Monceaux24歲。他於1271年與Alice De La Hyde Lady在英國萊希斯特斯爾加索普結婚。他們至少是3個兒子的父母。他去世於1287年7月21日在英國諾廷加姆爾拉可頓享年31歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭諾廷加姆爾拉可頓。

nameMeanings.name: de
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Dilip, Anirban, Gopal, Alok, Anindya, Arunava, Asit, Asok, Dibyendu, Goutam, Indra, Indranil.

variants.name: Dey
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Indian Ashit, Indu, Naina, Partha, Soumya, Ajit, Anjan, Anjana, Anup, Arjun, Arun, Ashim.
English, Scottish, and Welsh: variant of   Day  .
German: topographic name from a field name in North Rhine-Westphalia, denoting a sizeable piece of land, from Middle Low German dē ‘thigh’ (referring to the shape of the property).

nameMeanings.name: Everingham
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (East Yorkshire): habitational name from Everingham (East Yorkshire), named in Old English Yferingaham ‘homestead (hām) of the people (-inga-) of Eofor’.

nameMeanings.name: Robert
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: One of the many French names of Germanic origin that were introduced into Britain by the Normans; it has since remained in continuous use. It is derived from the nearly synonymous elements hrōd ‘fame’ + berht ‘bright, famous’, and had a native Old English predecessor of similar form (Hreodbeorht), which was supplanted by the Norman name. Two dukes of Normandy in the 11th century bore the name: the father of William the Conqueror (sometimes identified with the legendary Robert the Devil), and his eldest son. It was borne also by three kings of Scotland, notably Robert the Bruce ( 1274–1329 ), who freed Scotland from English domination. The altered short form Bob is very common, but Hob and Dob, which were common in the Middle Ages and gave rise to surnames, are extinct. See also Rupert .

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Robert de Everingham II was born on 9 February 1256, in Fairburn, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, Adam de Everingham I, was 26 and his mother, Maude de Monceaux, was 24. He married Alice De La Hyde Lady in 1271, in Garthorpe, Leicestershire, England. They were the parents of at least 3 sons. He died on 21 July 1287, in Laxton, Nottinghamshire, England, at the age of 31, and was buried in Laxton, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Robert d’Everingham II est né le 9 février 1256, à Fairburn, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, Adam d’Everingham I, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Maude de Monceaux, avait 24 ans. Il a épousé Alice De La Hyde Lady en 1271, à Garthorpe, Leicestershire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils. Il est décédé le 21 juillet 1287, à Laxton, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 31 ans, et a été enterré à Laxton, Nottinghamshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

de.lifeSummary: Als Robert de Everingham II am 9. Februar 1256 in Fairburn, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater Adam de Everingham I 26 und seine Mutter Maude de Monceaux 24. Er hat 1271 in Garthorpe, Leicestershire, England Alice De La Hyde Lady geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne. Er starb am 21. Juli 1287 im Alter von 31 Jahren in Laxton, Nottinghamshire, England und wurde in Laxton, Nottinghamshire, England, United Kingdom begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Everingham II è nato il il 9 febbraio 1256 nel Fairburn, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, Adam de Everingham I, aveva 26 anni e il suo madre, Maude de Monceaux, aveva 24 anni. Lui sposato Alice De La Hyde Lady nel 1271, nel Garthorpe, Leicestershire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli. Morì il 21 luglio 1287, nel Laxton, Nottinghamshire, England, all'età di 31 anni, e fu sepolto a Laxton, Nottinghamshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito.

ja.lifeSummary: Robert de Everingham IIが1256年2月9日にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアファイルブルンで生まれた時，父Adam de Everingham Iは26歳，母Maude de Monceauxは24歳であった。1271年にイギリスレアイセステルシュアイアガーソルペでAlice De La Hyde Ladyと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子の親であった。彼は1287年7月21日にイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアラクストンで31歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスノッティンガムシュアイアラクストンにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Robert de Everingham II(이)가 1256년 2월 9일 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 파어번에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Adam de Everingham I(은)는 26세였고, 그의 어머니 Maude de Monceaux(은)는 24세였다. 그는 1271년 영국 레이스스터실르 가소에서 Alice De La Hyde Lady(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1287년 7월 21일 영국 노틴그함실르 라스턴에서 31세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 노틴그함실르 라스턴에 묻혔다.

pt.9 February 1256–21 July 1287: 9 de fevereiro de 1256–21 de julho de 1287
pt.lifeSummary: Quando Robert de Everingham II nasceu em 9 de fevereiro de 1256, em Fairburn, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, Adam de Everingham I, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Maude de Monceaux, tinha 24 anos. Ele casou-se com Alice De La Hyde Lady em 1271, em Garthorpe, Leicestershire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos. Ele faleceu em 21 de julho de 1287, em Laxton, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, com 31 anos, e foi sepultado em Laxton, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Robert de Everingham II родился 9 февраля 1256 г. в местности феирбурн, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени Adam de Everingham I было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Maude de Monceaux, 24. Он женился на женщине по имени Alice De La Hyde Lady в 1271 г. в местности гартаурп, лисестиршир, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына. Он умер 21 июля 1287 г. в местности лакстон, ноттингхамшир, Англия в возрасте 31 год и был похоронен в местности лакстон, ноттингхамшир, Англия, Великобритания.

es.9 February 1256–21 July 1287: 9 de febrero de 1256–21 de julio de 1287
es.lifeSummary: Cuando Robert de Everingham II nació el 9 de febrero de 1256, en Fairburn, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, Adam de Everingham I, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Maude de Monceaux, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Alice De La Hyde Lady en 1271, en Garthorpe, Leicestershire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos. Murió el 21 de julio de 1287, en Laxton, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 31 años, y fue sepultado en Laxton, Nottinghamshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido.
